name: Work story (/work skill)
content:
You are executing the /work skill on {{runtime.story_id}}. Operate fully autonomously in auto mode: do not pause for plan approval, do not ask the user mid-flow, do not stop until the story is Done or you hit a hard failure you cannot resolve.

Procedure (must follow .agents/skills/work/SKILL.md):
1. Locate the story file by globbing `.s_e_e/stories/stories/{{runtime.story_id}} - *.md`. If zero or multiple matches, abort and report.
2. Read the story file. Capture Description, every `- [ ] #N` AC, Implementation Plan, and frontmatter `status` / `dependencies` / `labels`.
3. Abort if `status` is not `New` or `Ready for Dev`, or if any dependency story is not `Done`.
4. Edit the story frontmatter to `status: In Progress` and bump `updated_date` (format `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm'`). Do not touch HTML section markers.
5. Research: read every file the Implementation Plan references and skim the docs named by the story `labels` (e.g. `doc-2`, `doc-3`).
6. Implement in tight loops:
   - One focused change per iteration.
   - Add or update tests for that change.
   - Run `mise run build`; fix failures before proceeding.
   - Run the relevant targeted tests; fix failures before proceeding.
   - Flip the matching `- [ ] #N` to `- [x] #N` only after the AC is verified — never speculatively.
7. After substantive changes and before completion run `mise run quality`. If it fails, fix and rerun. Do not declare done while quality is red.
8. Fill `## Implementation Notes` (between `<!-- SECTION:NOTES:BEGIN -->` and `<!-- SECTION:NOTES:END -->`) with modified files, trade-offs, and key tests added. Fill `## Final Summary` (between its markers) with the outcome and verification (which tests passed, whether `mise run quality` passed, any caveats).
9. Set frontmatter `status: Done` and bump `updated_date`. Save.

Hard rules:
- Never proceed past a failing build or test.
- Every code change needs a test.
- Do not flip AC checkboxes you did not verify.
- Do not edit other stories or knowledge files unless an AC explicitly requires it.
- Keep code, comments, and assistant text terse.
